revision.text:
'''Yakumo Shrine''' (八雲神社) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} dedicated to {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王}} and {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}, associated with the {{ill|Gion faith|ja|祇園信仰}}. Other shrines related to the Gion faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} (also known as 八阪神社 or {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (multiple)|ja|弥栄神社}}), {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine|ja|広峯神社}}, {{ill|Tenno Shrine|ja|天王神社}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社}}, {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社}}, and {{ill|Kanshin Shrine|ja|感神社}}, among others, with the names varying by era and document.
The shrine's name derives from the {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}} verse recited by Susanoo: "Yakumo rises, the eightfold clouds enclose, in Izumo they build the eightfold fence to enclose my bride." The head shrine is {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|八坂神社}} in Kyoto.
Similar to other Susanoo-dedicated shrines, these were called "Gozu Tennō Shrines" until the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}. With the {{ill|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離}}, the name "Gozu Tennō" was prohibited, and the deity was reinterpreted as Susanoo, leading to renaming of these shrines accordingly.
== Notable Yakumo Shrines == Shrines dedicated to Susanoo under the name Yakumo Shrine can be found across Japan. Examples include:
* {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Yakumo Town)|ja|八雲神社 (八雲町)}} ({{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} {{ill|Yakumo Town|ja|八雲町}}) — a branch of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. * {{ill|Tenno-gu Yakumo Shrine|ja|天王宮八雲神社}} ({{ill|Sakata City|ja|酒田市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ichinoseki)|ja|八雲神社 (一関市)}} ({{ill|Ichinoseki|ja|一関市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kōriyama)|ja|八雲神社 (郡山市)}} ({{ill|Kōriyama|ja|郡山市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}) — one of seven Yakumo Shrines in the city; located alongside the worship hall of {{ill|Unagiro Wake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社}}, a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ashikaga)|ja|八雲神社 (足利市)}} ({{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}) — five Yakumo Shrines exist in the city. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Nasukarasuyama)|ja|八雲神社 (那須烏山市)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Nasukarasuyama|ja|那須烏山市}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Motegi)|ja|八雲神社 (茂木町)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Motegi|ja|茂木町}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Takasaki)|ja|八雲神社 (高崎市)}} ({{ill|Takasaki|ja|高崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Koga)|ja|八雲神社 (笠間市)}} ({{ill|Koga|ja|笠間市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kuki)|ja|八雲神社 (久喜市)}} ({{ill|Kuki|ja|久喜市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}) ...
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Gion faith|ja|祇園信仰}} * {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} * {{ill|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離}}

revision.text:
'''Yakumo Shrine''' (八雲神社) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} dedicated to {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王}} and {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}, associated with the {{ill|Gion faith|ja|祇園信仰}}. Other shrines related to the Gion faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} (also known as 八阪神社 or {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (multiple)|ja|弥栄神社}}), {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine|ja|広峯神社}}, {{ill|Tenno Shrine|ja|天王神社}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社}}, {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社}}, and {{ill|Kanshin Shrine|ja|感神社}}, among others, with the names varying by era and document.
The shrine's name derives from the {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}} verse recited by Susanoo: "Yakumo rises, the eightfold clouds enclose, in Izumo they build the eightfold fence to enclose my bride." The head shrine is {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|八坂神社}} in Kyoto.
Similar to other Susanoo-dedicated shrines, these were called "Gozu Tennō Shrines" until the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}. With the {{ill|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離}}, the name "Gozu Tennō" was prohibited, and the deity was reinterpreted as Susanoo, leading to renaming of these shrines accordingly.
== Notable Yakumo Shrines == Shrines dedicated to Susanoo under the name Yakumo Shrine can be found across Japan. Examples include:
* {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Yakumo Town)|ja|八雲神社 (八雲町)}} ({{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} {{ill|Yakumo Town|ja|八雲町}}) — a branch of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. * {{ill|Tenno-gu Yakumo Shrine|ja|天王宮八雲神社}} ({{ill|Sakata City|ja|酒田市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ichinoseki)|ja|八雲神社 (一関市)}} ({{ill|Ichinoseki|ja|一関市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kōriyama)|ja|八雲神社 (郡山市)}} ({{ill|Kōriyama|ja|郡山市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}) — one of seven Yakumo Shrines in the city; located alongside the worship hall of {{ill|Unagiro Wake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社}}, a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ashikaga)|ja|八雲神社 (足利市)}} ({{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}) — five Yakumo Shrines exist in the city. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Nasukarasuyama)|ja|八雲神社 (那須烏山市)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Nasukarasuyama|ja|那須烏山市}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Motegi)|ja|八雲神社 (茂木町)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Motegi|ja|茂木町}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Takasaki)|ja|八雲神社 (高崎市)}} ({{ill|Takasaki|ja|高崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Koga)|ja|八雲神社 (笠間市)}} ({{ill|Koga|ja|笠間市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kuki)|ja|八雲神社 (久喜市)}} ({{ill|Kuki|ja|久喜市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}) ...
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Gion faith|ja|祇園信仰}} * {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} * {{ill|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離}}

'''Yakumo Shrine''' (八雲神社) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} dedicated to {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王}} and {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}, associated with the {{ill|Gion faith|ja|祇園信仰}}. Other shrines related to the Gion faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} (also known as 八阪神社 or {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (multiple)|ja|弥栄神社}}), {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine|ja|広峯神社}}, {{ill|Tenno Shrine|ja|天王神社}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社}}, {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社}}, and {{ill|Kanshin Shrine|ja|感神社}}, among others, with the names varying by era and document.
The shrine's name derives from the {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}} verse recited by Susanoo: "Yakumo rises, the eightfold clouds enclose, in Izumo they build the eightfold fence to enclose my bride." The head shrine is {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|八坂神社}} in Kyoto.
Similar to other Susanoo-dedicated shrines, these were called "Gozu Tennō Shrines" until the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}. With the {{ill|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離}}, the name "Gozu Tennō" was prohibited, and the deity was reinterpreted as Susanoo, leading to renaming of these shrines accordingly.
== Notable Yakumo Shrines == Shrines dedicated to Susanoo under the name Yakumo Shrine can be found across Japan. Examples include:
* {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Yakumo Town)|ja|八雲神社 (八雲町)}} ({{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} {{ill|Yakumo Town|ja|八雲町}}) — a branch of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. * {{ill|Tenno-gu Yakumo Shrine|ja|天王宮八雲神社}} ({{ill|Sakata City|ja|酒田市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ichinoseki)|ja|八雲神社 (一関市)}} ({{ill|Ichinoseki|ja|一関市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kōriyama)|ja|八雲神社 (郡山市)}} ({{ill|Kōriyama|ja|郡山市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}) — one of seven Yakumo Shrines in the city; located alongside the worship hall of {{ill|Unagiro Wake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社}}, a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ashikaga)|ja|八雲神社 (足利市)}} ({{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}) — five Yakumo Shrines exist in the city. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Nasukarasuyama)|ja|八雲神社 (那須烏山市)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Nasukarasuyama|ja|那須烏山市}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Motegi)|ja|八雲神社 (茂木町)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Motegi|ja|茂木町}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Takasaki)|ja|八雲神社 (高崎市)}} ({{ill|Takasaki|ja|高崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Koga)|ja|八雲神社 (笠間市)}} ({{ill|Koga|ja|笠間市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kuki)|ja|八雲神社 (久喜市)}} ({{ill|Kuki|ja|久喜市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}) ...

'''Yakumo Shrine''' (八雲神社) is a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]] dedicated to {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王}} and {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}, associated with the {{ill|Gion faith|ja|祇園信仰}}. Other shrines related to the Gion faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} (also known as 八阪神社 or {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (multiple)|ja|弥栄神社}}), {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine|ja|広峯神社}}, {{ill|Tenno Shrine|ja|天王神社}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社}}, {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社}}, and {{ill|Kanshin Shrine|ja|感神社}}, among others, with the names varying by era and document.
The shrine's name derives from the {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}} verse recited by Susanoo: "Yakumo rises, the eightfold clouds enclose, in Izumo they build the eightfold fence to enclose my bride." The head shrine is {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|八坂神社}} in Kyoto.
Similar to other Susanoo-dedicated shrines, these were called "Gozu Tennō Shrines" until the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}. With the {{ill|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離}}, the name "Gozu Tennō" was prohibited, and the deity was reinterpreted as Susanoo, leading to renaming of these shrines accordingly.
== Notable Yakumo Shrines == Shrines dedicated to Susanoo under the name Yakumo Shrine can be found across Japan. Examples include:
* {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Yakumo Town)|ja|八雲神社 (八雲町)}} ({{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} {{ill|Yakumo Town|ja|八雲町}}) — a branch of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. * {{ill|Tenno-gu Yakumo Shrine|ja|天王宮八雲神社}} ({{ill|Sakata City|ja|酒田市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ichinoseki)|ja|八雲神社 (一関市)}} ({{ill|Ichinoseki|ja|一関市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kōriyama)|ja|八雲神社 (郡山市)}} ({{ill|Kōriyama|ja|郡山市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}) — one of seven Yakumo Shrines in the city; located alongside the worship hall of {{ill|Unagiro Wake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社}}, a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ashikaga)|ja|八雲神社 (足利市)}} ({{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}) — five Yakumo Shrines exist in the city. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Nasukarasuyama)|ja|八雲神社 (那須烏山市)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Nasukarasuyama|ja|那須烏山市}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Motegi)|ja|八雲神社 (茂木町)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Motegi|ja|茂木町}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Takasaki)|ja|八雲神社 (高崎市)}} ({{ill|Takasaki|ja|高崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Koga)|ja|八雲神社 (笠間市)}} ({{ill|Koga|ja|笠間市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kuki)|ja|八雲神社 (久喜市)}} ({{ill|Kuki|ja|久喜市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}) ...

'''Yakumo Shrine''' (八雲神社) is a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]] dedicated to {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王}} and {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}, associated with the {{ill|Gion faith|ja|祇園信仰}}. Other shrines related to the Gion faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} (also known as 八阪神社 or {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (multiple)|ja|弥栄神社}}), {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine|ja|広峯神社}}, {{ill|Tenno Shrine|ja|天王神社}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社}}, {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社}}, and {{ill|Kanshin Shrine|ja|感神社}}, among others, with the names varying by era and document.
The shrine's name derives from the {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}} verse recited by Susanoo: "Yakumo rises, the eightfold clouds enclose, in Izumo they build the eightfold fence to enclose my bride." The head shrine is {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|八坂神社}} in Kyoto.
Similar to other Susanoo-dedicated shrines, these were called "Gozu Tennō Shrines" until the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}. With the {{ill|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離}}, the name "Gozu Tennō" was prohibited, and the deity was reinterpreted as Susanoo, leading to renaming of these shrines accordingly.
== Notable Yakumo Shrines == Shrines dedicated to Susanoo under the name Yakumo Shrine can be found across Japan. Examples include:
* {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Yakumo Town)|ja|八雲神社 (八雲町)}} ({{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} {{ill|Yakumo Town|ja|八雲町}}) — a branch of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. * {{ill|Tenno-gu Yakumo Shrine|ja|天王宮八雲神社}} ({{ill|Sakata City|ja|酒田市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ichinoseki)|ja|八雲神社 (一関市)}} ({{ill|Ichinoseki|ja|一関市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kōriyama)|ja|八雲神社 (郡山市)}} ({{ill|Kōriyama|ja|郡山市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}) — one of seven Yakumo Shrines in the city; located alongside the worship hall of {{ill|Unagiro Wake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社}}, a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ashikaga)|ja|八雲神社 (足利市)}} ({{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}) — five Yakumo Shrines exist in the city. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Nasukarasuyama)|ja|八雲神社 (那須烏山市)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Nasukarasuyama|ja|那須烏山市}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Motegi)|ja|八雲神社 (茂木町)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Motegi|ja|茂木町}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Takasaki)|ja|八雲神社 (高崎市)}} ({{ill|Takasaki|ja|高崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Koga)|ja|八雲神社 (笠間市)}} ({{ill|Koga|ja|笠間市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kuki)|ja|八雲神社 (久喜市)}} ({{ill|Kuki|ja|久喜市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}) ...

'''Yakumo Shrine''' (八雲神社) is a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]] dedicated to {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王}} and {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}, associated with the {{ill|Gion faith|ja|祇園信仰}}. Other shrines related to the Gion faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} (also known as 八阪神社 or {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (multiple)|ja|弥栄神社}}), {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine|ja|広峯神社}}, {{ill|Tenno Shrine|ja|天王神社}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社}}, {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社}}, and {{ill|Kanshin Shrine|ja|感神社}}, among others, with the names varying by era and document.
The shrine's name derives from the {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}} verse recited by Susanoo: "Yakumo rises, the eightfold clouds enclose, in Izumo they build the eightfold fence to enclose my bride." The head shrine is {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|八坂神社}} in Kyoto.
Similar to other Susanoo-dedicated shrines, these were called "Gozu Tennō Shrines" until the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}. With the {{ill|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離}}, the name "Gozu Tennō" was prohibited, and the deity was reinterpreted as Susanoo, leading to renaming of these shrines accordingly.
== Notable Yakumo Shrines == Shrines dedicated to Susanoo under the name Yakumo Shrine can be found across Japan. Examples include:
* {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Yakumo Town)|ja|八雲神社 (八雲町)}} ({{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} {{ill|Yakumo Town|ja|八雲町}}) — a branch of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. * {{ill|Tenno-gu Yakumo Shrine|ja|天王宮八雲神社}} ({{ill|Sakata City|ja|酒田市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ichinoseki)|ja|八雲神社 (一関市)}} ({{ill|Ichinoseki|ja|一関市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kōriyama)|ja|八雲神社 (郡山市)}} ({{ill|Kōriyama|ja|郡山市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}) — one of seven Yakumo Shrines in the city; located alongside the worship hall of {{ill|Unagiro Wake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社}}, a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ashikaga)|ja|八雲神社 (足利市)}} ({{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}) — five Yakumo Shrines exist in the city. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Nasukarasuyama)|ja|八雲神社 (那須烏山市)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Nasukarasuyama|ja|那須烏山市}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Motegi)|ja|八雲神社 (茂木町)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Motegi|ja|茂木町}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Takasaki)|ja|八雲神社 (高崎市)}} ({{ill|Takasaki|ja|高崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Koga)|ja|八雲神社 (笠間市)}} ({{ill|Koga|ja|笠間市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kuki)|ja|八雲神社 (久喜市)}} ({{ill|Kuki|ja|久喜市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}) ...

'''Yakumo Shrine''' (八雲神社) is a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]] dedicated to {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王}} and {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}, associated with the {{ill|Gion faith|ja|祇園信仰}}. Other shrines related to the Gion faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} (also known as 八阪神社 or {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (multiple)|ja|弥栄神社}}), {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine|ja|広峯神社}}, {{ill|Tenno Shrine|ja|天王神社}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社}}, {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社}}, and {{ill|Kanshin Shrine|ja|感神社}}, among others, with the names varying by era and document.
The shrine's name derives from the {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}} verse recited by Susanoo: "Yakumo rises, the eightfold clouds enclose, in Izumo they build the eightfold fence to enclose my bride." The head shrine is {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|八坂神社}} in Kyoto.
Similar to other Susanoo-dedicated shrines, these were called "Gozu Tennō Shrines" until the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}. With the {{ill|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離}}, the name "Gozu Tennō" was prohibited, and the deity was reinterpreted as Susanoo, leading to renaming of these shrines accordingly.
== Notable Yakumo Shrines == Shrines dedicated to Susanoo under the name Yakumo Shrine can be found across Japan. Examples include:
* {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Yakumo Town)|ja|八雲神社 (八雲町)}} ({{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} {{ill|Yakumo Town|ja|八雲町}}) — a branch of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. * {{ill|Tenno-gu Yakumo Shrine|ja|天王宮八雲神社}} ({{ill|Sakata City|ja|酒田市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ichinoseki)|ja|八雲神社 (一関市)}} ({{ill|Ichinoseki|ja|一関市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kōriyama)|ja|八雲神社 (郡山市)}} ({{ill|Kōriyama|ja|郡山市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}) — one of seven Yakumo Shrines in the city; located alongside the worship hall of {{ill|Unagiro Wake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社}}, a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ashikaga)|ja|八雲神社 (足利市)}} ({{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}) — five Yakumo Shrines exist in the city. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Nasukarasuyama)|ja|八雲神社 (那須烏山市)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Nasukarasuyama|ja|那須烏山市}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Motegi)|ja|八雲神社 (茂木町)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Motegi|ja|茂木町}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Takasaki)|ja|八雲神社 (高崎市)}} ({{ill|Takasaki|ja|高崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Koga)|ja|八雲神社 (笠間市)}} ({{ill|Koga|ja|笠間市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kuki)|ja|八雲神社 (久喜市)}} ({{ill|Kuki|ja|久喜市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}) ...

'''Yakumo Shrine''' (八雲神社) is a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]] dedicated to {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王}} and {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}, associated with the {{ill|Gion faith|ja|祇園信仰}}. Other shrines related to the Gion faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} (also known as 八阪神社 or {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (multiple)|ja|弥栄神社}}), {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine|ja|広峯神社}}, {{ill|Tenno Shrine|ja|天王神社}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社}}, {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社}}, and {{ill|Kanshin Shrine|ja|感神社}}, among others, with the names varying by era and document.
The shrine's name derives from the {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}} verse recited by Susanoo: "Yakumo rises, the eightfold clouds enclose, in Izumo they build the eightfold fence to enclose my bride." The head shrine is {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|八坂神社}} in Kyoto.
Similar to other Susanoo-dedicated shrines, these were called "Gozu Tennō Shrines" until the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}. With the {{ill|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離}}, the name "Gozu Tennō" was prohibited, and the deity was reinterpreted as Susanoo, leading to renaming of these shrines accordingly.
== Notable Yakumo Shrines == Shrines dedicated to Susanoo under the name Yakumo Shrine can be found across Japan. Examples include:
* {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Yakumo Town)|ja|八雲神社 (八雲町)}} ({{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} {{ill|Yakumo Town|ja|八雲町}}) — a branch of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. * {{ill|Tenno-gu Yakumo Shrine|ja|天王宮八雲神社}} ({{ill|Sakata City|ja|酒田市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ichinoseki)|ja|八雲神社 (一関市)}} ({{ill|Ichinoseki|ja|一関市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kōriyama)|ja|八雲神社 (郡山市)}} ({{ill|Kōriyama|ja|郡山市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}) — one of seven Yakumo Shrines in the city; located alongside the worship hall of {{ill|Unagiro Wake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社}}, a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ashikaga)|ja|八雲神社 (足利市)}} ({{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}) — five Yakumo Shrines exist in the city. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Nasukarasuyama)|ja|八雲神社 (那須烏山市)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Nasukarasuyama|ja|那須烏山市}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Motegi)|ja|八雲神社 (茂木町)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Motegi|ja|茂木町}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Takasaki)|ja|八雲神社 (高崎市)}} ({{ill|Takasaki|ja|高崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Koga)|ja|八雲神社 (笠間市)}} ({{ill|Koga|ja|笠間市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kuki)|ja|八雲神社 (久喜市)}} ({{ill|Kuki|ja|久喜市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}) ...

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] to [[Category:fix the O]]{{DEFAULTSORT{{subst:PAGENAME}}}}[[category:fix the O]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Yakumo Shrine''' (八雲神社) is a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]] dedicated to {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王}} and {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}, associated with the {{ill|Gion faith|ja|祇園信仰}}. Other shrines related to the Gion faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} (also known as 八阪神社 or {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (multiple)|ja|弥栄神社}}), {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine|ja|広峯神社}}, {{ill|Tenno Shrine|ja|天王神社}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社}}, {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社}}, and {{ill|Kanshin Shrine|ja|感神社}}, among others, with the names varying by era and document.
The shrine's name derives from the {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}} verse recited by Susanoo: "Yakumo rises, the eightfold clouds enclose, in Izumo they build the eightfold fence to enclose my bride." The head shrine is {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|八坂神社}} in Kyoto.
Similar to other Susanoo-dedicated shrines, these were called "Gozu Tennō Shrines" until the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}. With the {{ill|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離}}, the name "Gozu Tennō" was prohibited, and the deity was reinterpreted as Susanoo, leading to renaming of these shrines accordingly.
== Notable Yakumo Shrines == Shrines dedicated to Susanoo under the name Yakumo Shrine can be found across Japan. Examples include:
* {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Yakumo Town)|ja|八雲神社 (八雲町)}} ({{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} {{ill|Yakumo Town|ja|八雲町}}) — a branch of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. * {{ill|Tenno-gu Yakumo Shrine|ja|天王宮八雲神社}} ({{ill|Sakata City|ja|酒田市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ichinoseki)|ja|八雲神社 (一関市)}} ({{ill|Ichinoseki|ja|一関市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kōriyama)|ja|八雲神社 (郡山市)}} ({{ill|Kōriyama|ja|郡山市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}) — one of seven Yakumo Shrines in the city; located alongside the worship hall of {{ill|Unagiro Wake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社}}, a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ashikaga)|ja|八雲神社 (足利市)}} ({{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}) — five Yakumo Shrines exist in the city. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Nasukarasuyama)|ja|八雲神社 (那須烏山市)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Nasukarasuyama|ja|那須烏山市}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Motegi)|ja|八雲神社 (茂木町)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Motegi|ja|茂木町}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Takasaki)|ja|八雲神社 (高崎市)}} ({{ill|Takasaki|ja|高崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Koga)|ja|八雲神社 (笠間市)}} ({{ill|Koga|ja|笠間市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kuki)|ja|八雲神社 (久喜市)}} ({{ill|Kuki|ja|久喜市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}) ...
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Gion faith|ja|祇園信仰}} * {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} * {{ill|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離}}

* {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Yakumo Town)|ja|八雲神社 (八雲町)}} ({{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} {{ill|Yakumo Town|ja|八雲町}}) — a branch of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. * {{ill|Tenno-gu Yakumo Shrine|ja|天王宮八雲神社}} ({{ill|Sakata City|ja|酒田市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ichinoseki)|ja|八雲神社 (一関市)}} ({{ill|Ichinoseki|ja|一関市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kōriyama)|ja|八雲神社 (郡山市)}} ({{ill|Kōriyama|ja|郡山市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}) — one of seven Yakumo Shrines in the city; located alongside the worship hall of {{ill|Unagiro Wake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社}}, a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ashikaga)|ja|八雲神社 (足利市)}} ({{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}) — five Yakumo Shrines exist in the city. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Nasukarasuyama)|ja|八雲神社 (那須烏山市)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Nasukarasuyama|ja|那須烏山市}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Motegi)|ja|八雲神社 (茂木町)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Motegi|ja|茂木町}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Takasaki)|ja|八雲神社 (高崎市)}} ({{ill|Takasaki|ja|高崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Koga)|ja|八雲神社 (笠間市)}} ({{ill|Koga|ja|笠間市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kuki)|ja|八雲神社 (久喜市)}} ({{ill|Kuki|ja|久喜市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}) ...

'''Yakumo Shrine''' (八雲神社) is a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]] dedicated to {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王}} and {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}, associated with the {{ill|Gion faith|ja|祇園信仰}}. Other shrines related to the Gion faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} (also known as 八阪神社 or {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (multiple)|ja|弥栄神社}}), {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine|ja|広峯神社}}, {{ill|Tenno Shrine|ja|天王神社}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社}}, {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社}}, and {{ill|Kanshin Shrine|ja|感神社}}, among others, with the names varying by era and document.
The shrine's name derives from the {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}} verse recited by Susanoo: "Yakumo rises, the eightfold clouds enclose, in Izumo they build the eightfold fence to enclose my bride." The head shrine is {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|八坂神社}} in Kyoto.
Similar to other Susanoo-dedicated shrines, these were called "Gozu Tennō Shrines" until the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}. With the {{ill|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離}}, the name "Gozu Tennō" was prohibited, and the deity was reinterpreted as Susanoo, leading to renaming of these shrines accordingly.
== Notable Yakumo Shrines == Shrines dedicated to Susanoo under the name Yakumo Shrine can be found across Japan. Examples include:
* {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Yakumo Town)|ja|八雲神社 (八雲町)}} ({{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} {{ill|Yakumo Town|ja|八雲町}}) — a branch of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. * {{ill|Tenno-gu Yakumo Shrine|ja|天王宮八雲神社}} ({{ill|Sakata City|ja|酒田市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ichinoseki)|ja|八雲神社 (一関市)}} ({{ill|Ichinoseki|ja|一関市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kōriyama)|ja|八雲神社 (郡山市)}} ({{ill|Kōriyama|ja|郡山市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}) — one of seven Yakumo Shrines in the city; located alongside the worship hall of {{ill|Unagiro Wake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社}}, a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ashikaga)|ja|八雲神社 (足利市)}} ({{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}) — five Yakumo Shrines exist in the city. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Nasukarasuyama)|ja|八雲神社 (那須烏山市)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Nasukarasuyama|ja|那須烏山市}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Motegi)|ja|八雲神社 (茂木町)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Motegi|ja|茂木町}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Takasaki)|ja|八雲神社 (高崎市)}} ({{ill|Takasaki|ja|高崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Koga)|ja|八雲神社 (笠間市)}} ({{ill|Koga|ja|笠間市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kuki)|ja|八雲神社 (久喜市)}} ({{ill|Kuki|ja|久喜市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}) ...

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: attributing pages to myself
revision.text:
'''Yakumo Shrine''' (八雲神社) is a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]] dedicated to {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王}} and {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}, associated with the {{ill|Gion faith|ja|祇園信仰}}. Other shrines related to the Gion faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} (also known as 八阪神社 or {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (multiple)|ja|弥栄神社}}), {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine|ja|広峯神社}}, {{ill|Tenno Shrine|ja|天王神社}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社}}, {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社}}, and {{ill|Kanshin Shrine|ja|感神社}}, among others, with the names varying by era and document.
The shrine's name derives from the {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}} verse recited by Susanoo: "Yakumo rises, the eightfold clouds enclose, in Izumo they build the eightfold fence to enclose my bride." The head shrine is {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|八坂神社}} in Kyoto.
Similar to other Susanoo-dedicated shrines, these were called "Gozu Tennō Shrines" until the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}. With the {{ill|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離}}, the name "Gozu Tennō" was prohibited, and the deity was reinterpreted as Susanoo, leading to renaming of these shrines accordingly.
== Notable Yakumo Shrines == Shrines dedicated to Susanoo under the name Yakumo Shrine can be found across Japan. Examples include:
* {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Yakumo Town)|ja|八雲神社 (八雲町)}} ({{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} {{ill|Yakumo Town|ja|八雲町}}) — a branch of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. * {{ill|Tenno-gu Yakumo Shrine|ja|天王宮八雲神社}} ({{ill|Sakata City|ja|酒田市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ichinoseki)|ja|八雲神社 (一関市)}} ({{ill|Ichinoseki|ja|一関市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kōriyama)|ja|八雲神社 (郡山市)}} ({{ill|Kōriyama|ja|郡山市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}) — one of seven Yakumo Shrines in the city; located alongside the worship hall of {{ill|Unagiro Wake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社}}, a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ashikaga)|ja|八雲神社 (足利市)}} ({{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}) — five Yakumo Shrines exist in the city. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Nasukarasuyama)|ja|八雲神社 (那須烏山市)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Nasukarasuyama|ja|那須烏山市}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Motegi)|ja|八雲神社 (茂木町)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Motegi|ja|茂木町}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Takasaki)|ja|八雲神社 (高崎市)}} ({{ill|Takasaki|ja|高崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Koga)|ja|八雲神社 (笠間市)}} ({{ill|Koga|ja|笠間市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kuki)|ja|八雲神社 (久喜市)}} ({{ill|Kuki|ja|久喜市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}) ...
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Gion faith|ja|祇園信仰}} * {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} * {{ill|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離}}

'''Yakumo Shrine''' (八雲神社) is a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]] dedicated to {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王}} and {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}, associated with the {{ill|Gion faith|ja|祇園信仰}}. Other shrines related to the Gion faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} (also known as 八阪神社 or {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (multiple)|ja|弥栄神社}}), {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine|ja|広峯神社}}, {{ill|Tenno Shrine|ja|天王神社}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社}}, {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社}}, and {{ill|Kanshin Shrine|ja|感神社}}, among others, with the names varying by era and document.
The shrine's name derives from the {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}} verse recited by Susanoo: "Yakumo rises, the eightfold clouds enclose, in Izumo they build the eightfold fence to enclose my bride." The head shrine is {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|八坂神社}} in Kyoto.
Similar to other Susanoo-dedicated shrines, these were called "Gozu Tennō Shrines" until the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}. With the {{ill|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離}}, the name "Gozu Tennō" was prohibited, and the deity was reinterpreted as Susanoo, leading to renaming of these shrines accordingly.
== Notable Yakumo Shrines == Shrines dedicated to Susanoo under the name Yakumo Shrine can be found across Japan. Examples include:
* {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Yakumo Town)|ja|八雲神社 (八雲町)}} ({{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} {{ill|Yakumo Town|ja|八雲町}}) — a branch of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. * {{ill|Tenno-gu Yakumo Shrine|ja|天王宮八雲神社}} ({{ill|Sakata City|ja|酒田市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ichinoseki)|ja|八雲神社 (一関市)}} ({{ill|Ichinoseki|ja|一関市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kōriyama)|ja|八雲神社 (郡山市)}} ({{ill|Kōriyama|ja|郡山市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}) — one of seven Yakumo Shrines in the city; located alongside the worship hall of {{ill|Unagiro Wake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社}}, a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ashikaga)|ja|八雲神社 (足利市)}} ({{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}) — five Yakumo Shrines exist in the city. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Nasukarasuyama)|ja|八雲神社 (那須烏山市)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Nasukarasuyama|ja|那須烏山市}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Motegi)|ja|八雲神社 (茂木町)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Motegi|ja|茂木町}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Takasaki)|ja|八雲神社 (高崎市)}} ({{ill|Takasaki|ja|高崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Koga)|ja|八雲神社 (笠間市)}} ({{ill|Koga|ja|笠間市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kuki)|ja|八雲神社 (久喜市)}} ({{ill|Kuki|ja|久喜市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}) ...
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Gion faith|ja|祇園信仰}} * {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} * {{ill|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離}}

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Yakumo Shrine''' (八雲神社) is a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]] dedicated to {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王}} and {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}, associated with the {{ill|Gion faith|ja|祇園信仰}}. Other shrines related to the Gion faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} (also known as 八阪神社 or {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (multiple)|ja|弥栄神社}}), {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine|ja|広峯神社}}, {{ill|Tenno Shrine|ja|天王神社}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社}}, {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社}}, and {{ill|Kanshin Shrine|ja|感神社}}, among others, with the names varying by era and document.
The shrine's name derives from the {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}} verse recited by Susanoo: "Yakumo rises, the eightfold clouds enclose, in Izumo they build the eightfold fence to enclose my bride." The head shrine is {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|八坂神社}} in Kyoto.
Similar to other Susanoo-dedicated shrines, these were called "Gozu Tennō Shrines" until the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}. With the {{ill|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離}}, the name "Gozu Tennō" was prohibited, and the deity was reinterpreted as Susanoo, leading to renaming of these shrines accordingly.
== Notable Yakumo Shrines == Shrines dedicated to Susanoo under the name Yakumo Shrine can be found across Japan. Examples include:
* {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Yakumo Town)|ja|八雲神社 (八雲町)}} ({{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} {{ill|Yakumo Town|ja|八雲町}}) — a branch of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. * {{ill|Tenno-gu Yakumo Shrine|ja|天王宮八雲神社}} ({{ill|Sakata City|ja|酒田市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ichinoseki)|ja|八雲神社 (一関市)}} ({{ill|Ichinoseki|ja|一関市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kōriyama)|ja|八雲神社 (郡山市)}} ({{ill|Kōriyama|ja|郡山市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}) — one of seven Yakumo Shrines in the city; located alongside the worship hall of {{ill|Unagiro Wake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社}}, a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ashikaga)|ja|八雲神社 (足利市)}} ({{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}) — five Yakumo Shrines exist in the city. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Nasukarasuyama)|ja|八雲神社 (那須烏山市)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Nasukarasuyama|ja|那須烏山市}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Motegi)|ja|八雲神社 (茂木町)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Motegi|ja|茂木町}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Takasaki)|ja|八雲神社 (高崎市)}} ({{ill|Takasaki|ja|高崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Koga)|ja|八雲神社 (笠間市)}} ({{ill|Koga|ja|笠間市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kuki)|ja|八雲神社 (久喜市)}} ({{ill|Kuki|ja|久喜市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}) ...
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Gion faith|ja|祇園信仰}} * {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} * {{ill|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離}}

* {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Yakumo Town)|ja|八雲神社 (八雲町)}} ({{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} {{ill|Yakumo Town|ja|八雲町}}) — a branch of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. * {{ill|Tenno-gu Yakumo Shrine|ja|天王宮八雲神社}} ({{ill|Sakata City|ja|酒田市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ichinoseki)|ja|八雲神社 (一関市)}} ({{ill|Ichinoseki|ja|一関市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kōriyama)|ja|八雲神社 (郡山市)}} ({{ill|Kōriyama|ja|郡山市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}) — one of seven Yakumo Shrines in the city; located alongside the worship hall of {{ill|Unagiro Wake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社}}, a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ashikaga)|ja|八雲神社 (足利市)}} ({{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}) — five Yakumo Shrines exist in the city. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Nasukarasuyama)|ja|八雲神社 (那須烏山市)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Nasukarasuyama|ja|那須烏山市}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Motegi)|ja|八雲神社 (茂木町)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Motegi|ja|茂木町}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Takasaki)|ja|八雲神社 (高崎市)}} ({{ill|Takasaki|ja|高崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Koga)|ja|八雲神社 (笠間市)}} ({{ill|Koga|ja|笠間市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kuki)|ja|八雲神社 (久喜市)}} ({{ill|Kuki|ja|久喜市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}) ...

'''Yakumo Shrine''' (八雲神社) is a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]] dedicated to {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王}} and {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}, associated with the {{ill|Gion faith|ja|祇園信仰}}. Other shrines related to the Gion faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} (also known as 八阪神社 or {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (multiple)|ja|弥栄神社}}), {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine|ja|広峯神社}}, {{ill|Tenno Shrine|ja|天王神社}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社}}, {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社}}, and {{ill|Kanshin Shrine|ja|感神社}}, among others, with the names varying by era and document.
The shrine's name derives from the {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}} verse recited by Susanoo: "Yakumo rises, the eightfold clouds enclose, in Izumo they build the eightfold fence to enclose my bride." The head shrine is {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|八坂神社}} in Kyoto.
Similar to other Susanoo-dedicated shrines, these were called "Gozu Tennō Shrines" until the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}. With the {{ill|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離}}, the name "Gozu Tennō" was prohibited, and the deity was reinterpreted as Susanoo, leading to renaming of these shrines accordingly.
== Notable Yakumo Shrines == Shrines dedicated to Susanoo under the name Yakumo Shrine can be found across Japan. Examples include:
* {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Yakumo Town)|ja|八雲神社 (八雲町)}} ({{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} {{ill|Yakumo Town|ja|八雲町}}) — a branch of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. * {{ill|Tenno-gu Yakumo Shrine|ja|天王宮八雲神社}} ({{ill|Sakata City|ja|酒田市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ichinoseki)|ja|八雲神社 (一関市)}} ({{ill|Ichinoseki|ja|一関市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kōriyama)|ja|八雲神社 (郡山市)}} ({{ill|Kōriyama|ja|郡山市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}) — one of seven Yakumo Shrines in the city; located alongside the worship hall of {{ill|Unagiro Wake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社}}, a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ashikaga)|ja|八雲神社 (足利市)}} ({{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}) — five Yakumo Shrines exist in the city. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Nasukarasuyama)|ja|八雲神社 (那須烏山市)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Nasukarasuyama|ja|那須烏山市}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Motegi)|ja|八雲神社 (茂木町)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Motegi|ja|茂木町}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Takasaki)|ja|八雲神社 (高崎市)}} ({{ill|Takasaki|ja|高崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Koga)|ja|八雲神社 (笠間市)}} ({{ill|Koga|ja|笠間市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kuki)|ja|八雲神社 (久喜市)}} ({{ill|Kuki|ja|久喜市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}) ...

The shrine's name derives from the {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}} verse recited by Susanoo: "Yakumo rises, the eightfold clouds enclose, in Izumo they build the eightfold fence to enclose my bride." The head shrine is {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|八坂神社}} in Kyoto.
Similar to other Susanoo-dedicated shrines, these were called "Gozu Tennō Shrines" until the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}. With the {{ill|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離}}, the name "Gozu Tennō" was prohibited, and the deity was reinterpreted as Susanoo, leading to renaming of these shrines accordingly.
== Notable Yakumo Shrines == Shrines dedicated to Susanoo under the name Yakumo Shrine can be found across Japan. Examples include:
* {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Yakumo Town)|ja|八雲神社 (八雲町)}} ({{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} {{ill|Yakumo Town|ja|八雲町}}) — a branch of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. * {{ill|Tenno-gu Yakumo Shrine|ja|天王宮八雲神社}} ({{ill|Sakata City|ja|酒田市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ichinoseki)|ja|八雲神社 (一関市)}} ({{ill|Ichinoseki|ja|一関市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kōriyama)|ja|八雲神社 (郡山市)}} ({{ill|Kōriyama|ja|郡山市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}) — one of seven Yakumo Shrines in the city; located alongside the worship hall of {{ill|Unagiro Wake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社}}, a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ashikaga)|ja|八雲神社 (足利市)}} ({{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}) — five Yakumo Shrines exist in the city. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Nasukarasuyama)|ja|八雲神社 (那須烏山市)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Nasukarasuyama|ja|那須烏山市}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Motegi)|ja|八雲神社 (茂木町)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Motegi|ja|茂木町}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Takasaki)|ja|八雲神社 (高崎市)}} ({{ill|Takasaki|ja|高崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Koga)|ja|八雲神社 (笠間市)}} ({{ill|Koga|ja|笠間市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kuki)|ja|八雲神社 (久喜市)}} ({{ill|Kuki|ja|久喜市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}) ...
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Gion faith|ja|祇園信仰}} * {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} * {{ill|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離}}

'''Yakumo Shrine''' (八雲神社) is a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]] dedicated to {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王}} and {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}, associated with the {{ill|Gion faith|ja|祇園信仰}}. Other shrines related to the Gion faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} (also known as 八阪神社 or {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (multiple)|ja|弥栄神社}}), {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine|ja|広峯神社}}, {{ill|Tenno Shrine|ja|天王神社}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社}}, {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社}}, and {{ill|Kanshin Shrine|ja|感神社}}, among others, with the names varying by era and document.
The shrine's name derives from the {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}} verse recited by Susanoo: "Yakumo rises, the eightfold clouds enclose, in Izumo they build the eightfold fence to enclose my bride." The head shrine is {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|八坂神社}} in Kyoto.
Similar to other Susanoo-dedicated shrines, these were called "Gozu Tennō Shrines" until the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}. With the {{ill|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離}}, the name "Gozu Tennō" was prohibited, and the deity was reinterpreted as Susanoo, leading to renaming of these shrines accordingly.
== Notable Yakumo Shrines == Shrines dedicated to Susanoo under the name Yakumo Shrine can be found across Japan. Examples include:
* {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Yakumo Town)|ja|八雲神社 (八雲町)}} ({{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} {{ill|Yakumo Town|ja|八雲町}}) — a branch of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. * {{ill|Tenno-gu Yakumo Shrine|ja|天王宮八雲神社}} ({{ill|Sakata City|ja|酒田市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ichinoseki)|ja|八雲神社 (一関市)}} ({{ill|Ichinoseki|ja|一関市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kōriyama)|ja|八雲神社 (郡山市)}} ({{ill|Kōriyama|ja|郡山市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}) — one of seven Yakumo Shrines in the city; located alongside the worship hall of {{ill|Unagiro Wake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社}}, a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ashikaga)|ja|八雲神社 (足利市)}} ({{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}) — five Yakumo Shrines exist in the city. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Nasukarasuyama)|ja|八雲神社 (那須烏山市)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Nasukarasuyama|ja|那須烏山市}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Motegi)|ja|八雲神社 (茂木町)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Motegi|ja|茂木町}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Takasaki)|ja|八雲神社 (高崎市)}} ({{ill|Takasaki|ja|高崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Koga)|ja|八雲神社 (笠間市)}} ({{ill|Koga|ja|笠間市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kuki)|ja|八雲神社 (久喜市)}} ({{ill|Kuki|ja|久喜市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}) ...
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Gion faith|ja|祇園信仰}} * {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} * {{ill|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離}}

revision.text:
'''Yakumo Shrine''' (八雲神社) is a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]] dedicated to {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王}} and {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}, associated with the {{ill|Gion faith|ja|祇園信仰}}. Other shrines related to the Gion faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} (also known as 八阪神社 or {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (multiple)|ja|弥栄神社}}), {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine|ja|広峯神社}}, {{ill|Tenno Shrine|ja|天王神社}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社}}, {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社}}, and {{ill|Kanshin Shrine|ja|感神社}}, among others, with the names varying by era and document.
The shrine's name derives from the {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}} verse recited by Susanoo: "Yakumo rises, the eightfold clouds enclose, in Izumo they build the eightfold fence to enclose my bride." The head shrine is {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|八坂神社}} in Kyoto.
Similar to other Susanoo-dedicated shrines, these were called "Gozu Tennō Shrines" until the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}. With the {{ill|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離}}, the name "Gozu Tennō" was prohibited, and the deity was reinterpreted as Susanoo, leading to renaming of these shrines accordingly.
== Notable Yakumo Shrines == Shrines dedicated to Susanoo under the name Yakumo Shrine can be found across Japan. Examples include:
* {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Yakumo Town)|ja|八雲神社 (八雲町)}} ({{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} {{ill|Yakumo Town|ja|八雲町}}) — a branch of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. * {{ill|Tenno-gu Yakumo Shrine|ja|天王宮八雲神社}} ({{ill|Sakata City|ja|酒田市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ichinoseki)|ja|八雲神社 (一関市)}} ({{ill|Ichinoseki|ja|一関市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kōriyama)|ja|八雲神社 (郡山市)}} ({{ill|Kōriyama|ja|郡山市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}) — one of seven Yakumo Shrines in the city; located alongside the worship hall of {{ill|Unagiro Wake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社}}, a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ashikaga)|ja|八雲神社 (足利市)}} ({{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}) — five Yakumo Shrines exist in the city. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Nasukarasuyama)|ja|八雲神社 (那須烏山市)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Nasukarasuyama|ja|那須烏山市}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Motegi)|ja|八雲神社 (茂木町)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Motegi|ja|茂木町}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Takasaki)|ja|八雲神社 (高崎市)}} ({{ill|Takasaki|ja|高崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Koga)|ja|八雲神社 (笠間市)}} ({{ill|Koga|ja|笠間市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kuki)|ja|八雲神社 (久喜市)}} ({{ill|Kuki|ja|久喜市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}) ...
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Gion faith|ja|祇園信仰}} * {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} * {{ill|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離}}

[[ja:八雲神社]]

'''Yakumo Shrine''' (八雲神社) is a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]] dedicated to {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王}} and {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}, associated with the {{ill|Gion faith|ja|祇園信仰}}. Other shrines related to the Gion faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} (also known as 八阪神社 or {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (multiple)|ja|弥栄神社}}), {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine|ja|広峯神社}}, {{ill|Tenno Shrine|ja|天王神社}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社}}, {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社}}, and {{ill|Kanshin Shrine|ja|感神社}}, among others, with the names varying by era and document.
The shrine's name derives from the {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}} verse recited by Susanoo: "Yakumo rises, the eightfold clouds enclose, in Izumo they build the eightfold fence to enclose my bride." The head shrine is {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|八坂神社}} in Kyoto.
Similar to other Susanoo-dedicated shrines, these were called "Gozu Tennō Shrines" until the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}. With the {{ill|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離}}, the name "Gozu Tennō" was prohibited, and the deity was reinterpreted as Susanoo, leading to renaming of these shrines accordingly.
== Notable Yakumo Shrines == Shrines dedicated to Susanoo under the name Yakumo Shrine can be found across Japan. Examples include:
* {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Yakumo Town)|ja|八雲神社 (八雲町)}} ({{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} {{ill|Yakumo Town|ja|八雲町}}) — a branch of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. * {{ill|Tenno-gu Yakumo Shrine|ja|天王宮八雲神社}} ({{ill|Sakata City|ja|酒田市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ichinoseki)|ja|八雲神社 (一関市)}} ({{ill|Ichinoseki|ja|一関市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kōriyama)|ja|八雲神社 (郡山市)}} ({{ill|Kōriyama|ja|郡山市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}) — one of seven Yakumo Shrines in the city; located alongside the worship hall of {{ill|Unagiro Wake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社}}, a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ashikaga)|ja|八雲神社 (足利市)}} ({{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}) — five Yakumo Shrines exist in the city. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Nasukarasuyama)|ja|八雲神社 (那須烏山市)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Nasukarasuyama|ja|那須烏山市}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Motegi)|ja|八雲神社 (茂木町)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Motegi|ja|茂木町}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Takasaki)|ja|八雲神社 (高崎市)}} ({{ill|Takasaki|ja|高崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Koga)|ja|八雲神社 (笠間市)}} ({{ill|Koga|ja|笠間市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kuki)|ja|八雲神社 (久喜市)}} ({{ill|Kuki|ja|久喜市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}) ...

'''Yakumo Shrine''' (八雲神社) is a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]] dedicated to {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王}} and {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}, associated with the {{ill|Gion faith|ja|祇園信仰}}. Other shrines related to the Gion faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} (also known as 八阪神社 or {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (multiple)|ja|弥栄神社}}), {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine|ja|広峯神社}}, {{ill|Tenno Shrine|ja|天王神社}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社}}, {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社}}, and {{ill|Kanshin Shrine|ja|感神社}}, among others, with the names varying by era and document.
The shrine's name derives from the {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}} verse recited by Susanoo: "Yakumo rises, the eightfold clouds enclose, in Izumo they build the eightfold fence to enclose my bride." The head shrine is {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|八坂神社}} in Kyoto.
Similar to other Susanoo-dedicated shrines, these were called "Gozu Tennō Shrines" until the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}. With the {{ill|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離}}, the name "Gozu Tennō" was prohibited, and the deity was reinterpreted as Susanoo, leading to renaming of these shrines accordingly.
== Notable Yakumo Shrines == Shrines dedicated to Susanoo under the name Yakumo Shrine can be found across Japan. Examples include:
* {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Yakumo Town)|ja|八雲神社 (八雲町)}} ({{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} {{ill|Yakumo Town|ja|八雲町}}) — a branch of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. * {{ill|Tenno-gu Yakumo Shrine|ja|天王宮八雲神社}} ({{ill|Sakata City|ja|酒田市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ichinoseki)|ja|八雲神社 (一関市)}} ({{ill|Ichinoseki|ja|一関市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kōriyama)|ja|八雲神社 (郡山市)}} ({{ill|Kōriyama|ja|郡山市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}) — one of seven Yakumo Shrines in the city; located alongside the worship hall of {{ill|Unagiro Wake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社}}, a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ashikaga)|ja|八雲神社 (足利市)}} ({{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}) — five Yakumo Shrines exist in the city. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Nasukarasuyama)|ja|八雲神社 (那須烏山市)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Nasukarasuyama|ja|那須烏山市}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Motegi)|ja|八雲神社 (茂木町)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Motegi|ja|茂木町}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Takasaki)|ja|八雲神社 (高崎市)}} ({{ill|Takasaki|ja|高崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Koga)|ja|八雲神社 (笠間市)}} ({{ill|Koga|ja|笠間市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kuki)|ja|八雲神社 (久喜市)}} ({{ill|Kuki|ja|久喜市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}) ...

'''Yakumo Shrine''' (八雲神社) is a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]] dedicated to {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王}} and {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}, associated with the {{ill|Gion faith|ja|祇園信仰}}. Other shrines related to the Gion faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} (also known as 八阪神社 or {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (multiple)|ja|弥栄神社}}), {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine|ja|広峯神社}}, {{ill|Tenno Shrine|ja|天王神社}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社}}, {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社}}, and {{ill|Kanshin Shrine|ja|感神社}}, among others, with the names varying by era and document.
The shrine's name derives from the {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}} verse recited by Susanoo: "Yakumo rises, the eightfold clouds enclose, in Izumo they build the eightfold fence to enclose my bride." The head shrine is {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|八坂神社}} in Kyoto.
Similar to other Susanoo-dedicated shrines, these were called "Gozu Tennō Shrines" until the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}. With the {{ill|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離}}, the name "Gozu Tennō" was prohibited, and the deity was reinterpreted as Susanoo, leading to renaming of these shrines accordingly.

* {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Yakumo Town)|ja|八雲神社 (八雲町)}} ({{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} {{ill|Yakumo Town|ja|八雲町}}) — a branch of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. * {{ill|Tenno-gu Yakumo Shrine|ja|天王宮八雲神社}} ({{ill|Sakata City|ja|酒田市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ichinoseki)|ja|八雲神社 (一関市)}} ({{ill|Ichinoseki|ja|一関市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kōriyama)|ja|八雲神社 (郡山市)}} ({{ill|Kōriyama|ja|郡山市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}) — one of seven Yakumo Shrines in the city; located alongside the worship hall of {{ill|Unagiro Wake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社}}, a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ashikaga)|ja|八雲神社 (足利市)}} ({{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}) — five Yakumo Shrines exist in the city. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Nasukarasuyama)|ja|八雲神社 (那須烏山市)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Nasukarasuyama|ja|那須烏山市}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Motegi)|ja|八雲神社 (茂木町)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Motegi|ja|茂木町}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Takasaki)|ja|八雲神社 (高崎市)}} ({{ill|Takasaki|ja|高崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Koga)|ja|八雲神社 (笠間市)}} ({{ill|Koga|ja|笠間市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kuki)|ja|八雲神社 (久喜市)}} ({{ill|Kuki|ja|久喜市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}) ...

'''Yakumo Shrine''' (八雲神社) is a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]] dedicated to {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王}} and {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}, associated with the {{ill|Gion faith|ja|祇園信仰}}. Other shrines related to the Gion faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} (also known as 八阪神社 or {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (multiple)|ja|弥栄神社}}), {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine|ja|広峯神社}}, {{ill|Tenno Shrine|ja|天王神社}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社}}, {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社}}, and {{ill|Kanshin Shrine|ja|感神社}}, among others, with the names varying by era and document.
The shrine's name derives from the {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}} verse recited by Susanoo: "Yakumo rises, the eightfold clouds enclose, in Izumo they build the eightfold fence to enclose my bride." The head shrine is {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|八坂神社}} in Kyoto.
Similar to other Susanoo-dedicated shrines, these were called "Gozu Tennō Shrines" until the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}. With the {{ill|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離}}, the name "Gozu Tennō" was prohibited, and the deity was reinterpreted as Susanoo, leading to renaming of these shrines accordingly.
== Notable Yakumo Shrines == Shrines dedicated to Susanoo under the name Yakumo Shrine can be found across Japan. Examples include:
* {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Yakumo Town)|ja|八雲神社 (八雲町)}} ({{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} {{ill|Yakumo Town|ja|八雲町}}) — a branch of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. * {{ill|Tenno-gu Yakumo Shrine|ja|天王宮八雲神社}} ({{ill|Sakata City|ja|酒田市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ichinoseki)|ja|八雲神社 (一関市)}} ({{ill|Ichinoseki|ja|一関市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kōriyama)|ja|八雲神社 (郡山市)}} ({{ill|Kōriyama|ja|郡山市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}) — one of seven Yakumo Shrines in the city; located alongside the worship hall of {{ill|Unagiro Wake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社}}, a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ashikaga)|ja|八雲神社 (足利市)}} ({{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}) — five Yakumo Shrines exist in the city. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Nasukarasuyama)|ja|八雲神社 (那須烏山市)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Nasukarasuyama|ja|那須烏山市}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Motegi)|ja|八雲神社 (茂木町)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Motegi|ja|茂木町}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Takasaki)|ja|八雲神社 (高崎市)}} ({{ill|Takasaki|ja|高崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Koga)|ja|八雲神社 (笠間市)}} ({{ill|Koga|ja|笠間市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kuki)|ja|八雲神社 (久喜市)}} ({{ill|Kuki|ja|久喜市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}) ...

[[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:q--1]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Yakumo Shrine}][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]] [[ja:八雲神社]]

'''Yakumo Shrine''' (八雲神社) is a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]] dedicated to {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王}} and {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}, associated with the {{ill|Gion faith|ja|祇園信仰}}. Other shrines related to the Gion faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} (also known as 八阪神社 or {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (multiple)|ja|弥栄神社}}), {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine|ja|広峯神社}}, {{ill|Tenno Shrine|ja|天王神社}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社}}, {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社}}, and {{ill|Kanshin Shrine|ja|感神社}}, among others, with the names varying by era and document.
The shrine's name derives from the {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}} verse recited by Susanoo: "Yakumo rises, the eightfold clouds enclose, in Izumo they build the eightfold fence to enclose my bride." The head shrine is {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|八坂神社}} in Kyoto.
Similar to other Susanoo-dedicated shrines, these were called "Gozu Tennō Shrines" until the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}. With the {{ill|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離}}, the name "Gozu Tennō" was prohibited, and the deity was reinterpreted as Susanoo, leading to renaming of these shrines accordingly.
== Notable Yakumo Shrines == Shrines dedicated to Susanoo under the name Yakumo Shrine can be found across Japan. Examples include:
* {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Yakumo Town)|ja|八雲神社 (八雲町)}} ({{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} {{ill|Yakumo Town|ja|八雲町}}) — a branch of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. * {{ill|Tenno-gu Yakumo Shrine|ja|天王宮八雲神社}} ({{ill|Sakata City|ja|酒田市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ichinoseki)|ja|八雲神社 (一関市)}} ({{ill|Ichinoseki|ja|一関市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kōriyama)|ja|八雲神社 (郡山市)}} ({{ill|Kōriyama|ja|郡山市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}) — one of seven Yakumo Shrines in the city; located alongside the worship hall of {{ill|Unagiro Wake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社}}, a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ashikaga)|ja|八雲神社 (足利市)}} ({{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}) — five Yakumo Shrines exist in the city. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Nasukarasuyama)|ja|八雲神社 (那須烏山市)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Nasukarasuyama|ja|那須烏山市}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Motegi)|ja|八雲神社 (茂木町)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Motegi|ja|茂木町}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Takasaki)|ja|八雲神社 (高崎市)}} ({{ill|Takasaki|ja|高崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Koga)|ja|八雲神社 (笠間市)}} ({{ill|Koga|ja|笠間市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kuki)|ja|八雲神社 (久喜市)}} ({{ill|Kuki|ja|久喜市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}) ...

'''Yakumo Shrine''' (八雲神社) is a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]] dedicated to {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王}} and {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}, associated with the {{ill|Gion faith|ja|祇園信仰}}. Other shrines related to the Gion faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} (also known as 八阪神社 or {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (multiple)|ja|弥栄神社}}), {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine|ja|広峯神社}}, {{ill|Tenno Shrine|ja|天王神社}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社}}, {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社}}, and {{ill|Kanshin Shrine|ja|感神社}}, among others, with the names varying by era and document.
The shrine's name derives from the {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}} verse recited by Susanoo: "Yakumo rises, the eightfold clouds enclose, in Izumo they build the eightfold fence to enclose my bride." The head shrine is {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|八坂神社}} in Kyoto.
Similar to other Susanoo-dedicated shrines, these were called "Gozu Tennō Shrines" until the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}. With the {{ill|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離}}, the name "Gozu Tennō" was prohibited, and the deity was reinterpreted as Susanoo, leading to renaming of these shrines accordingly.
== Notable Yakumo Shrines == Shrines dedicated to Susanoo under the name Yakumo Shrine can be found across Japan. Examples include:
* {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Yakumo Town)|ja|八雲神社 (八雲町)}} ({{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} {{ill|Yakumo Town|ja|八雲町}}) — a branch of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. * {{ill|Tenno-gu Yakumo Shrine|ja|天王宮八雲神社}} ({{ill|Sakata City|ja|酒田市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ichinoseki)|ja|八雲神社 (一関市)}} ({{ill|Ichinoseki|ja|一関市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kōriyama)|ja|八雲神社 (郡山市)}} ({{ill|Kōriyama|ja|郡山市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}) — one of seven Yakumo Shrines in the city; located alongside the worship hall of {{ill|Unagiro Wake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社}}, a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ashikaga)|ja|八雲神社 (足利市)}} ({{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}) — five Yakumo Shrines exist in the city. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Nasukarasuyama)|ja|八雲神社 (那須烏山市)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Nasukarasuyama|ja|那須烏山市}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Motegi)|ja|八雲神社 (茂木町)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Motegi|ja|茂木町}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Takasaki)|ja|八雲神社 (高崎市)}} ({{ill|Takasaki|ja|高崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Koga)|ja|八雲神社 (笠間市)}} ({{ill|Koga|ja|笠間市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kuki)|ja|八雲神社 (久喜市)}} ({{ill|Kuki|ja|久喜市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}) ...

'''Yakumo Shrine''' (八雲神社) is a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]] dedicated to {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王}} and {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}, associated with the {{ill|Gion faith|ja|祇園信仰}}. Other shrines related to the Gion faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} (also known as 八阪神社 or {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (multiple)|ja|弥栄神社}}), {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine|ja|広峯神社}}, {{ill|Tenno Shrine|ja|天王神社}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社}}, {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社}}, and {{ill|Kanshin Shrine|ja|感神社}}, among others, with the names varying by era and document.
The shrine's name derives from the {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}} verse recited by Susanoo: "Yakumo rises, the eightfold clouds enclose, in Izumo they build the eightfold fence to enclose my bride." The head shrine is {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|八坂神社}} in Kyoto.
Similar to other Susanoo-dedicated shrines, these were called "Gozu Tennō Shrines" until the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}. With the {{ill|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離}}, the name "Gozu Tennō" was prohibited, and the deity was reinterpreted as Susanoo, leading to renaming of these shrines accordingly.
== Notable Yakumo Shrines == Shrines dedicated to Susanoo under the name Yakumo Shrine can be found across Japan. Examples include:
* {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Yakumo Town)|ja|八雲神社 (八雲町)}} ({{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} {{ill|Yakumo Town|ja|八雲町}}) — a branch of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. * {{ill|Tenno-gu Yakumo Shrine|ja|天王宮八雲神社}} ({{ill|Sakata City|ja|酒田市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ichinoseki)|ja|八雲神社 (一関市)}} ({{ill|Ichinoseki|ja|一関市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kōriyama)|ja|八雲神社 (郡山市)}} ({{ill|Kōriyama|ja|郡山市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}) — one of seven Yakumo Shrines in the city; located alongside the worship hall of {{ill|Unagiro Wake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社}}, a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ashikaga)|ja|八雲神社 (足利市)}} ({{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}) — five Yakumo Shrines exist in the city. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Nasukarasuyama)|ja|八雲神社 (那須烏山市)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Nasukarasuyama|ja|那須烏山市}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Motegi)|ja|八雲神社 (茂木町)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Motegi|ja|茂木町}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Takasaki)|ja|八雲神社 (高崎市)}} ({{ill|Takasaki|ja|高崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Koga)|ja|八雲神社 (笠間市)}} ({{ill|Koga|ja|笠間市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kuki)|ja|八雲神社 (久喜市)}} ({{ill|Kuki|ja|久喜市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}) ...

'''Yakumo Shrine''' (八雲神社) is a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]] dedicated to {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王}} and {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}, associated with the {{ill|Gion faith|ja|祇園信仰}}. Other shrines related to the Gion faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} (also known as 八阪神社 or {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (multiple)|ja|弥栄神社}}), {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine|ja|広峯神社}}, {{ill|Tenno Shrine|ja|天王神社}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社}}, {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社}}, and {{ill|Kanshin Shrine|ja|感神社}}, among others, with the names varying by era and document.
The shrine's name derives from the {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}} verse recited by Susanoo: "Yakumo rises, the eightfold clouds enclose, in Izumo they build the eightfold fence to enclose my bride." The head shrine is {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|八坂神社}} in Kyoto.
Similar to other Susanoo-dedicated shrines, these were called "Gozu Tennō Shrines" until the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}. With the {{ill|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離}}, the name "Gozu Tennō" was prohibited, and the deity was reinterpreted as Susanoo, leading to renaming of these shrines accordingly.
== Notable Yakumo Shrines == Shrines dedicated to Susanoo under the name Yakumo Shrine can be found across Japan. Examples include:
* {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Yakumo Town)|ja|八雲神社 (八雲町)}} ({{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} {{ill|Yakumo Town|ja|八雲町}}) — a branch of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. * {{ill|Tenno-gu Yakumo Shrine|ja|天王宮八雲神社}} ({{ill|Sakata City|ja|酒田市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ichinoseki)|ja|八雲神社 (一関市)}} ({{ill|Ichinoseki|ja|一関市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kōriyama)|ja|八雲神社 (郡山市)}} ({{ill|Kōriyama|ja|郡山市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}) — one of seven Yakumo Shrines in the city; located alongside the worship hall of {{ill|Unagiro Wake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社}}, a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ashikaga)|ja|八雲神社 (足利市)}} ({{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}) — five Yakumo Shrines exist in the city. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Nasukarasuyama)|ja|八雲神社 (那須烏山市)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Nasukarasuyama|ja|那須烏山市}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Motegi)|ja|八雲神社 (茂木町)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Motegi|ja|茂木町}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Takasaki)|ja|八雲神社 (高崎市)}} ({{ill|Takasaki|ja|高崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Koga)|ja|八雲神社 (笠間市)}} ({{ill|Koga|ja|笠間市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kuki)|ja|八雲神社 (久喜市)}} ({{ill|Kuki|ja|久喜市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}) ...

'''Yakumo Shrine''' (八雲神社) is a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]] dedicated to {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王}} and {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}, associated with the {{ill|Gion faith|ja|祇園信仰}}. Other shrines related to the Gion faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} (also known as 八阪神社 or {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (multiple)|ja|弥栄神社}}), {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine|ja|広峯神社}}, {{ill|Tenno Shrine|ja|天王神社}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社}}, {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社}}, and {{ill|Kanshin Shrine|ja|感神社}}, among others, with the names varying by era and document.
The shrine's name derives from the {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}} verse recited by Susanoo: "Yakumo rises, the eightfold clouds enclose, in Izumo they build the eightfold fence to enclose my bride." The head shrine is {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|八坂神社}} in Kyoto.
Similar to other Susanoo-dedicated shrines, these were called "Gozu Tennō Shrines" until the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}. With the {{ill|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離}}, the name "Gozu Tennō" was prohibited, and the deity was reinterpreted as Susanoo, leading to renaming of these shrines accordingly.
== Notable Yakumo Shrines == Shrines dedicated to Susanoo under the name Yakumo Shrine can be found across Japan. Examples include:
* {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Yakumo Town)|ja|八雲神社 (八雲町)}} ({{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} {{ill|Yakumo Town|ja|八雲町}}) — a branch of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. * {{ill|Tenno-gu Yakumo Shrine|ja|天王宮八雲神社}} ({{ill|Sakata City|ja|酒田市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ichinoseki)|ja|八雲神社 (一関市)}} ({{ill|Ichinoseki|ja|一関市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kōriyama)|ja|八雲神社 (郡山市)}} ({{ill|Kōriyama|ja|郡山市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}) — one of seven Yakumo Shrines in the city; located alongside the worship hall of {{ill|Unagiro Wake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社}}, a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ashikaga)|ja|八雲神社 (足利市)}} ({{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}) — five Yakumo Shrines exist in the city. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Nasukarasuyama)|ja|八雲神社 (那須烏山市)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Nasukarasuyama|ja|那須烏山市}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Motegi)|ja|八雲神社 (茂木町)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Motegi|ja|茂木町}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Takasaki)|ja|八雲神社 (高崎市)}} ({{ill|Takasaki|ja|高崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Koga)|ja|八雲神社 (笠間市)}} ({{ill|Koga|ja|笠間市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kuki)|ja|八雲神社 (久喜市)}} ({{ill|Kuki|ja|久喜市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}) ...

With the {{ill|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離}}, the name "Gozu Tennō" was prohibited, and the deity was reinterpreted as Susanoo, leading to renaming of these shrines accordingly.

* {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Yakumo Town)|ja|八雲神社 (八雲町)}} ({{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} {{ill|Yakumo Town|ja|八雲町}}) — a branch of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. * {{ill|Tenno-gu Yakumo Shrine|ja|天王宮八雲神社}} ({{ill|Sakata City|ja|酒田市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ichinoseki)|ja|八雲神社 (一関市)}} ({{ill|Ichinoseki|ja|一関市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kōriyama)|ja|八雲神社 (郡山市)}} ({{ill|Kōriyama|ja|郡山市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}) — one of seven Yakumo Shrines in the city; located alongside the worship hall of {{ill|Unagiro Wake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社}}, a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ashikaga)|ja|八雲神社 (足利市)}} ({{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}) — five Yakumo Shrines exist in the city. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Nasukarasuyama)|ja|八雲神社 (那須烏山市)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Nasukarasuyama|ja|那須烏山市}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Motegi)|ja|八雲神社 (茂木町)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Motegi|ja|茂木町}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Takasaki)|ja|八雲神社 (高崎市)}} ({{ill|Takasaki|ja|高崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Koga)|ja|八雲神社 (笠間市)}} ({{ill|Koga|ja|笠間市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kuki)|ja|八雲神社 (久喜市)}} ({{ill|Kuki|ja|久喜市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}) ...

'''Yakumo Shrine''' (八雲神社) is a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]] dedicated to {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王}} and {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}, associated with the {{ill|Gion faith|ja|祇園信仰}}. Other shrines related to the Gion faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} (also known as 八阪神社 or {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (multiple)|ja|弥栄神社}}), {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine|ja|広峯神社}}, {{ill|Tenno Shrine|ja|天王神社}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社}}, {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社}}, and {{ill|Kanshin Shrine|ja|感神社}}, among others, with the names varying by era and document.
The shrine's name derives from the {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}} verse recited by Susanoo: "Yakumo rises, the eightfold clouds enclose, in Izumo they build the eightfold fence to enclose my bride." The head shrine is {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|八坂神社}} in Kyoto.
Similar to other Susanoo-dedicated shrines, these were called "Gozu Tennō Shrines" until the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}. With the {{ill|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離}}, the name "Gozu Tennō" was prohibited, and the deity was reinterpreted as Susanoo, leading to renaming of these shrines accordingly.
== Notable Yakumo Shrines == Shrines dedicated to Susanoo under the name Yakumo Shrine can be found across Japan. Examples include:
* {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Yakumo Town)|ja|八雲神社 (八雲町)}} ({{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} {{ill|Yakumo Town|ja|八雲町}}) — a branch of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. * {{ill|Tenno-gu Yakumo Shrine|ja|天王宮八雲神社}} ({{ill|Sakata City|ja|酒田市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ichinoseki)|ja|八雲神社 (一関市)}} ({{ill|Ichinoseki|ja|一関市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kōriyama)|ja|八雲神社 (郡山市)}} ({{ill|Kōriyama|ja|郡山市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}) — one of seven Yakumo Shrines in the city; located alongside the worship hall of {{ill|Unagiro Wake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社}}, a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ashikaga)|ja|八雲神社 (足利市)}} ({{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}) — five Yakumo Shrines exist in the city. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Nasukarasuyama)|ja|八雲神社 (那須烏山市)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Nasukarasuyama|ja|那須烏山市}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Motegi)|ja|八雲神社 (茂木町)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Motegi|ja|茂木町}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Takasaki)|ja|八雲神社 (高崎市)}} ({{ill|Takasaki|ja|高崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Koga)|ja|八雲神社 (笠間市)}} ({{ill|Koga|ja|笠間市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kuki)|ja|八雲神社 (久喜市)}} ({{ill|Kuki|ja|久喜市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}) ...

* {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Yakumo Town)|ja|八雲神社 (八雲町)}} ({{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} {{ill|Yakumo Town|ja|八雲町}}) — a branch of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. * {{ill|Tenno-gu Yakumo Shrine|ja|天王宮八雲神社}} ({{ill|Sakata City|ja|酒田市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ichinoseki)|ja|八雲神社 (一関市)}} ({{ill|Ichinoseki|ja|一関市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kōriyama)|ja|八雲神社 (郡山市)}} ({{ill|Kōriyama|ja|郡山市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}) — one of seven Yakumo Shrines in the city; located alongside the worship hall of {{ill|Unagiro Wake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社}}, a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ashikaga)|ja|八雲神社 (足利市)}} ({{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}) — five Yakumo Shrines exist in the city. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Nasukarasuyama)|ja|八雲神社 (那須烏山市)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Nasukarasuyama|ja|那須烏山市}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Motegi)|ja|八雲神社 (茂木町)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Motegi|ja|茂木町}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Takasaki)|ja|八雲神社 (高崎市)}} ({{ill|Takasaki|ja|高崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Koga)|ja|八雲神社 (笠間市)}} ({{ill|Koga|ja|笠間市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kuki)|ja|八雲神社 (久喜市)}} ({{ill|Kuki|ja|久喜市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}) ...

'''Yakumo Shrine''' (八雲神社) is a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]] dedicated to {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王}} and {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}, associated with the {{ill|Gion faith|ja|祇園信仰}}. Other shrines related to the Gion faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} (also known as 八阪神社 or {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (multiple)|ja|弥栄神社}}), {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine|ja|広峯神社}}, {{ill|Tenno Shrine|ja|天王神社}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社}}, {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社}}, and {{ill|Kanshin Shrine|ja|感神社}}, among others, with the names varying by era and document.
The shrine's name derives from the {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}} verse recited by Susanoo: "Yakumo rises, the eightfold clouds enclose, in Izumo they build the eightfold fence to enclose my bride." The head shrine is {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|八坂神社}} in Kyoto.
Similar to other Susanoo-dedicated shrines, these were called "Gozu Tennō Shrines" until the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}. With the {{ill|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離}}, the name "Gozu Tennō" was prohibited, and the deity was reinterpreted as Susanoo, leading to renaming of these shrines accordingly.
== Notable Yakumo Shrines == Shrines dedicated to Susanoo under the name Yakumo Shrine can be found across Japan. Examples include:
* {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Yakumo Town)|ja|八雲神社 (八雲町)}} ({{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} {{ill|Yakumo Town|ja|八雲町}}) — a branch of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. * {{ill|Tenno-gu Yakumo Shrine|ja|天王宮八雲神社}} ({{ill|Sakata City|ja|酒田市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ichinoseki)|ja|八雲神社 (一関市)}} ({{ill|Ichinoseki|ja|一関市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kōriyama)|ja|八雲神社 (郡山市)}} ({{ill|Kōriyama|ja|郡山市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}) — one of seven Yakumo Shrines in the city; located alongside the worship hall of {{ill|Unagiro Wake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社}}, a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Ashikaga)|ja|八雲神社 (足利市)}} ({{ill|Ashikaga|ja|足利市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}) — five Yakumo Shrines exist in the city. * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Nasukarasuyama)|ja|八雲神社 (那須烏山市)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Nasukarasuyama|ja|那須烏山市}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Motegi)|ja|八雲神社 (茂木町)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, {{ill|Motegi|ja|茂木町}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Takasaki)|ja|八雲神社 (高崎市)}} ({{ill|Takasaki|ja|高崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Koga)|ja|八雲神社 (笠間市)}} ({{ill|Koga|ja|笠間市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}) * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kuki)|ja|八雲神社 (久喜市)}} ({{ill|Kuki|ja|久喜市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}) ...
